methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Mazama is a Series A climate and energy technology company that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a peer set of 4 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside 1 public comparable and additional private benchmarks in the climate tech space.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $30M total raised as a proxy for post-money Series A valuation, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for progression to subsequent rounds, yielding $60M. Peer-median methodology isolates the 4 same-stage Climate & Energy comparables, which exhibit a median valuation of $175M (range: $110M to $1.8B), reflecting material dispersion driven largely by Watershed's $1.8B outlier valuation relative to peers clustered between $110M–$200M.

The triangulation supports a range of $51M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $118M. This range reflects tension between the conservatism of stage-progression mechanics ($60M) and the peer-median approach ($175M). Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of Watershed as a comparable given its $1.8B valuation—substantially above the peer cluster—and the limited visibility into Mazama's capital efficiency and growth trajectory relative to the benchmark set. The wide peer dispersion warrants scenario analysis anchored to the $110M–$150M band if Watershed is deemed an outlier.
